segments.text: As the deer pants for streams of water, so my soul pants for you, my God.
segments.speaker: Psalmist

footnotes: Maschil: or, A Psalm giving instruction of the sons
heading: A Longing for God

segments.text: My soul thirsts for God, for the living God. When can I go and meet with God?
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: My tears have been my food day and night, while people say to me all day long, 'Where is your God?'
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: These things I remember as I pour out my soul:
how I used to go to the house of God under the protection of the Mighty One with shouts of joy and praise among the festive throng.
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: Why, my soul, are you downcast? Why so disturbed within me? Put your hope in God, for I will yet praise him, my Savior and my God.
segments.speaker: Psalmist

footnotes: for the...: or, his presence is salvation

segments.text: My soul is downcast within me; therefore I will remember you from the land of the Jordan, the heights of Hermon—from Mount Mizar.
segments.speaker: Psalmist

footnotes: the hill...: or, the little hill

segments.text: Deep calls to deep in the roar of your waterfalls; all your waves and breakers have swept over me.
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: By day the Lord directs his love, at night his song is with me—a prayer to the God of my life.
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: I say to God my Rock, 'Why have you forgotten me? Why must I go about mourning, oppressed by the enemy?'
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: My bones suffer mortal agony as my foes taunt me, saying to me all day long, 'Where is your God?'
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: Why, my soul, are you downcast? Why so disturbed within me? Put your hope in God, for I will yet praise him, my Savior and my God.
segments.speaker: Psalmist
